subject: two location views of a haunted house interior and exterior — a cobweb-choked attic and a moonlit widow's walk balcony
description: A split-panel image showing two distinct locations within or attached to a gothic haunted house. The left panel depicts a ruined attic interior: the space is dense with cobwebs stretching between collapsed and angled wooden beams, lit by a single eerie blue glow emanating from a grimy window. The atmosphere is claustrophobic and abandoned. The right panel shows an elevated outdoor balcony or widow's walk at night: ornate wooden railings and overhead framework are visible, also draped with cobwebs. A small silhouetted figure stands or perches at the railing's far end. Blue-lit stained glass windows glow on the right side of the panel. The overall palette is dark amber and dusty gold in the right panel versus cold steel blue in the left, unified by darkness and decay.
foreground: collapsed wooden beams, dense cobwebs (left panel); ornate wooden railing with cobwebs (right panel)
background: glowing blue window in attic (left); stained glass windows and night sky (right)
emotion: desolate abandonment — emptiness and decay communicate isolation and dread without a visible face
